title: Tender Peppery Adventurous British (Cornish) Chestnut Mushroom Shepherd's Pie
description: A rich, peppery dish featuring chestnut mushrooms, inspired by traditional British Cornish cuisine. Perfect for a cozy dinner on a chilly evening.

ingredients.name: Red Wine (gluten-free option: red grape juice)

instructions: In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes. Set aside.
In the same skillet, add butter and sauté the diced onion until translucent, about 2-3 minutes. Add garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the beef stock and red wine to the skillet, scraping any browned bits from the bottom. Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes, reducing the liquid by half.
Stir in the gluten-free Worcestershire sauce, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute, then add the cooked mushrooms back into the skillet.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the heavy cream and grated cheddar cheese until smooth. Add the egg yolk and mix until well combined.
Stir the cream cheese mixture into the mushroom mixture in the skillet. Cook for 2-3 minutes, until the mixture is heated through.
Transfer the mixture to a greased 9x13-inch baking dish. Top with breadcrumbs, then drizzle with additional olive oil if desired.
To make the egg wash, whisk together the remaining egg yolk and a pinch of water. Brush the egg wash over the top of the crust.
Bake the shepherd's pie in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, until the crust is golden brown and the filling is hot and bubbly.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before serving. Garnish with chopped parsley and serve hot.
Cover and refrigerate leftovers for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
